steps.title: Read the histogram counts
steps.content:
Data set A has 50 values. Read each histogram bin and keep a running total:
  • 30 to under 40: 4 players; cumulative total 4
  • 40 to under 50: 7 more players; cumulative total 11
  • 50 to under 60: 27 more players; cumulative total 38
  • 60 to under 70: 12 more players; cumulative total 50
The 25th and 26th values both fall after cumulative total 11 and before cumulative total 38, so both are in the 50 to under 60 bin.

steps.title: Evaluate Statement I
steps.content:
For 50 values, the median of A is the average of the 25th and 26th values. After adding 18, data set B has 51 values, so its median is the 26th value of B.

Because 18 is smaller than every value in A, it becomes the first value in B. That means the 26th value of B is the old 25th value of A.

But the 25th and 26th values of A are both in the 50 to under 60 bin. They could be equal. If they are equal, the median does not decrease. Statement I is not guaranteed.

steps.title: Evaluate Statement II
steps.content: Every value in A is at least 30, so the mean of A must be greater than 18. Adding a new value below the current mean pulls the mean down. Since the added value is 18, the mean of B must be less than the mean of A. Statement II must be true.

steps.title: Combine the results
steps.content: Only Statement II is guaranteed, so only II must be true. That matches choice B — B is correct.
